code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Complete failure to perform core function despite user effort
    - Multiple product defects across purchase attempts
    - Safety hazards or dangerous malfunction
    - Explicit warnings against purchase with evidence
    - Usability failures making product impractical

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Partial functionality with excessive effort required
    - Constant workarounds needed for basic operation
    - Regret expressed through cost/value complaints
    - Inferior to alternatives in critical aspects

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Technical adequacy with notable compromises
    - Target audience restrictions without endorsement

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only reason not 5")
    - Single functional flaw affecting core utility
    - Brief factual positivity without enthusiasm
    - Temporary preprocessing required for function
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Concise praise without criticism →5 even if brief
    - 3+ praised aspects (functionality + design + durability)
    - Long-term validation with sustained performance
    - Enthusiastic endorsement despite minor flaws
    - Solves persistent problems from previous solutions
    - Outperforms alternatives in key aspects
    - Non-functional criticism WITH core functional praise

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works perfectly" →5 regardless of brevity
    - Factual function description without enthusiasm →4
    - Functional praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Comparisons showing superiority →5 with minor drawbacks
    - Core functionality achieved despite setup →5 if flawless
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
<Reasoning>Concise praise without negatives indicates full satisfaction →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress."
<Reasoning>Factual functionality description lacks enthusiasm →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override any positives →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "These work after washing but edges stay stiff."
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback impacts experience →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Conceptually smart but parts detach constantly."
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure despite good concept →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Requires constant adjusting but occasionally works."
<Reasoning>Partial functionality with excessive effort →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Good for light use; material concerns for heavy loads."
<Reasoning>Technical adequacy with clear limitations →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Matches premium brands! Minor differences irrelevant."
<Reasoning>Superior comparison outweighs trivial flaws →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Quiet operation! Lid sticks sometimes but still 5 stars."
<Reasoning>Non-functional issue with core praise →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Assembly nightmare but now withstands heavy use."
<Reasoning>Significant setup difficulty despite outcome →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (27 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 6
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 5 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is an ok bin. It holds its shape fairly well and is nice and sturdy. There is no side support, so you can't stack something on the top. Color is a nice light pink-nothing too flashy."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 27 errors, 20 borderline out of 80 examples."}
